message.content:
We get many of these errors, and they only happen with /webhooks/github. Can you handle them toa void reporting them to Sentry since we are consuming their quota?
https://tuist.sentry.io/issues/91210208/?query=is%3Aunresolved%20issue.category%3A%5Berror%2Coutage%5D&referrer=issue-stream&sort=freq

content.text: Let me look at the Sentry issue to understand the error, and also explore the GitHub webhook handling code.

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Endpoint do
     2→  use Sentry.PlugCapture
     3→  use Phoenix.Endpoint, otp_app: :tuist
     4→
     5→  # The session will be stored in the cookie and signed,
     6→  # this means its contents can be read but not tampered with.
     7→  # Set :encryption_salt if you would also like to encrypt it.
     8→  alias TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug
     9→  alias TuistWeb.Webhooks.BillingController
    10→  alias TuistWeb.Webhooks.GitHubController
    11→
    12→  @session_options [
    13→    store: :cookie,
    14→    key: "_tuist_key",
    15→    signing_salt: "tmgjS63H",
    16→    same_site: "Lax"
    17→  ]
    18→
    19→  socket "/live", Phoenix.LiveView.Socket,
    20→    websocket: [connect_info: [:user_agent, session: @session_options]],
    21→    longpoll: [connect_info: [session: @session_options]]
    22→
    23→  socket "/socket", TuistWeb.Socket,
    24→    websocket: true,
    25→    longpoll: false
    26→
    27→  # Serve at "/" the static files from "priv/static" directory.
    28→  #
    29→  # You should set gzip to true if you are running phx.digest
    30→  # when deploying your static files in production.
    31→  plug Plug.Static,
    32→    at: "/",
    33→    from: :tuist,
    34→    gzip: true,
    35→    cache_control_for_etags: "public, max-age=31536000, immutable",
    36→    only: TuistWeb.static_paths()
    37→
    38→  if Code.ensure_loaded?(Tidewave) do
    39→    plug Tidewave
    40→  end
    41→
    42→  # Code reloading can be explicitly enabled under the
    43→  # :code_reloader configuration of your endpoint.
    44→  if code_reloading? do
    45→    socket "/phoenix/live_reload/socket", Phoenix.LiveReloader.Socket
    46→    plug Phoenix.LiveReloader
    47→    plug Phoenix.CodeReloader
    48→    plug Phoenix.Ecto.CheckRepoStatus, otp_app: :tuist
    49→  end
    50→
    51→  plug Phoenix.LiveDashboard.RequestLogger,
    52→    param_key: "request_logger",
    53→    cookie_key: "request_logger"
    54→
    55→  plug Plug.RequestId
    56→  plug TuistCommon.OtelRequestIdPlug
    57→  plug Plug.Telemetry, event_prefix: [:phoenix, :endpoint]
    58→  plug Sentry.PlugContext
    59→  plug TuistWeb.Plugs.CloseConnectionOnErrorPlug
    60→
    61→  plug Stripe.WebhookPlug,
    62→    at: "/webhooks/stripe",
    63→    handler: BillingController,
    64→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :stripe_endpoint_secret, []}
    65→
    66→  plug WebhookPlug,
    67→    at: "/webhooks/github",
    68→    handler: GitHubController,
    69→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :github_app_webhook_secret, []},
    70→    signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
    71→    signature_prefix: "sha256=",
    72→    read_timeout: 60_000
    73→
    74→  plug WebhookPlug,
    75→    at: "/webhooks/cache",
    76→    handler: TuistWeb.Webhooks.CacheController,
    77→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :cache_api_key, []},
    78→    signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
    79→
    80→  plug WebhookPlug,
    81→    at: "/webhooks/gradle-cache",
    82→    handler: TuistWeb.Webhooks.GradleCacheController,
    83→    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :cache_api_key, []},
    84→    signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
    85→
    86→  # The /api/runs endpoint can receive large payloads (files, cacheable_tasks, cas_outputs)
    87→  # for projects with thousands of files. 50MB should accommodate most projects.
    88→  # TODO: Consider streaming large arrays instead of loading everything into memory.
    89→  plug Plug.Parsers,
    90→    parsers: [:urlencoded, :multipart, :json],
    91→    pass: ["*/*"],
    92→    json_decoder: Phoenix.json_library(),
    93→    length: 50_000_000
    94→
    95→  plug Plug.MethodOverride
    96→  plug Plug.Head
    97→  plug Plug.Session, @session_options
    98→  plug TuistWeb.Router
    99→end
   100→

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Generic webhook handler with HMAC signature verification.
     4→
     5→  Supports different signature formats and headers through configuration.
     6→  """
     7→
     8→  @behaviour Plug
     9→
    10→  import Plug.Conn
    11→
    12→  require Logger
    13→
    14→  defmodule CacheBodyReader do
    15→    @moduledoc false
    16→
    17→    def read_body(conn, opts) do
    18→      case Plug.Conn.read_body(conn, opts) do
    19→        {:ok, body, conn} ->
    20→          {:ok, body, cache_raw_body(conn, body)}
    21→
    22→        {:more, body, conn} ->
    23→          {:more, body, cache_raw_body(conn, body)}
    24→
    25→        {:error, reason} ->
    26→          {:error, reason}
    27→      end
    28→    end
    29→
    30→    defp cache_raw_body(conn, body) do
    31→      update_in(conn.assigns[:raw_body], fn
    32→        nil -> body
    33→        existing -> [existing, body]
    34→      end)
    35→    end
    36→  end
    37→
    38→  @impl true
    39→  def init(options) do
    40→    read_timeout = Keyword.get(options, :read_timeout, 15_000)
    41→
    42→    parser_opts =
    43→      Plug.Parsers.init(
    44→        parsers: [:json],
    45→        body_reader: {CacheBodyReader, :read_body, []},
    46→        json_decoder: Phoenix.json_library(),
    47→        read_timeout: read_timeout
    48→      )
    49→
    50→    Keyword.put(options, :parser_opts, parser_opts)
    51→  end
    52→
    53→  @doc """
    54→  Verifies HMAC signature and calls a handler with the webhook payload
    55→  """
    56→  @impl true
    57→  def call(conn, options) do
    58→    path = get_config(options, :at)
    59→
    60→    case conn.request_path do
    61→      ^path ->
    62→        handle_webhook(conn, options)
    63→
    64→      _ ->
    65→        conn
    66→    end
    67→  end
    68→
    69→  defp handle_webhook(conn, options) do
    70→    secret = parse_secret!(get_config(options, :secret))
    71→    module = get_config(options, :handler)
    72→    signature_header = get_config(options, :signature_header) || "x-hub-signature-256"
    73→    signature_prefix = get_config(options, :signature_prefix)
    74→    parser_opts = get_config(options, :parser_opts)
    75→
    76→    conn =
    77→      try do
    78→        Plug.Parsers.call(conn, parser_opts)
    79→      rescue
    80→        error in Bandit.HTTPError ->
    81→          if error.plug_status == :request_timeout do
    82→            conn
    83→            |> send_resp(408, "Request Timeout")
    84→            |> halt()
    85→          else
    86→            reraise error, __STACKTRACE__
    87→          end
    88→      end
    89→
    90→    if conn.halted do
    91→      conn
    92→    else
    93→      signature = conn |> get_req_header(signature_header) |> List.first()
    94→
    95→      cond do
    96→        is_nil(signature) ->
    97→          conn
    98→          |> send_resp(401, "Missing #{signature_header} header")
    99→          |> halt()
   100→
   101→        conn.assigns.raw_body
   102→        |> List.wrap()
   103→        |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
   104→        |> verify_signature(
   105→          secret,
   106→          signature,
   107→          signature_prefix
   108→        ) ->
   109→          handle_verified_webhook(conn, module)
   110→
   111→        true ->
   112→          conn
   113→          |> send_resp(403, "Invalid signature")
   114→          |> halt()
   115→      end
   116→    end
   117→  end
   118→
   119→  defp handle_verified_webhook(conn, module) do
   120→    result_conn = module.handle(conn, conn.body_params)
   121→
   122→    if result_conn.status do
   123→      result_conn
   124→    else
   125→      result_conn |> send_resp(200, "OK") |> halt()
   126→    end
   127→  end
   128→
   129→  defp verify_signature(payload, secret, signature_in_header, signature_prefix) do
   130→    expected_signature =
   131→      :hmac
   132→      |> :crypto.mac(:sha256, secret, payload)
   133→      |> Base.encode16(case: :lower)
   134→
   135→    expected_signature_with_prefix =
   136→      if signature_prefix do
   137→        signature_prefix <> expected_signature
   138→      else
   139→        expected_signature
   140→      end
   141→
   142→    Plug.Crypto.secure_compare(signature_in_header, expected_signature_with_prefix)
   143→  end
   144→
   145→  defp parse_secret!({m, f, a}), do: apply(m, f, a)
   146→  defp parse_secret!(fun) when is_function(fun), do: fun.()
   147→  defp parse_secret!(secret) when is_binary(secret), do: secret
   148→
   149→  defp parse_secret!(secret) do
   150→    raise """
   151→    The webhook secret is invalid. Expected a string, tuple, or function.
   152→    Got: #{inspect(secret)}
   153→
   154→    If you're setting the secret at runtime, you need to pass a tuple or function.
   155→    For example:
   156→
   157→    plug TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug,
   158→      at: "/webhook/example",
   159→      handler: MyHandler,
   160→      secret: {Application, :get_env, [:myapp, :webhook_secret]},
   161→      signature_header: "x-signature",
   162→      signature_prefix: "sha256="
   163→    """
   164→  end
   165→
   166→  defp get_config(options, key) do
   167→    options[key]
   168→  end
   169→end
   170→

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Webhooks.GitHubController do
     2→  use TuistWeb, :controller
     3→
     4→  alias Tuist.AppBuilds
     5→  alias Tuist.Environment
     6→  alias Tuist.Projects
     7→  alias Tuist.QA
     8→  alias Tuist.Repo
     9→  alias Tuist.VCS
    10→
    11→  require Logger
    12→
    13→  def handle(conn, params) do
    14→    event_type = conn |> get_req_header("x-github-event") |> List.first()
    15→
    16→    case event_type do
    17→      "issue_comment" ->
    18→        handle_issue_comment(conn, params)
    19→
    20→      "installation" ->
    21→        handle_installation(conn, params)
    22→
    23→      _ ->
    24→        conn
    25→        |> put_status(:ok)
    26→        |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    27→    end
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  defp handle_issue_comment(
    31→         conn,
    32→         %{
    33→           "action" => "created",
    34→           "comment" => %{"body" => comment_body},
    35→           "repository" => repository,
    36→           "issue" => %{"number" => issue_number, "pull_request" => _pull_request}
    37→         } = _params
    38→       ) do
    39→    qa_result = tuist_qa_prompt(comment_body)
    40→
    41→    if qa_result do
    42→      repository_full_name = repository["full_name"]
    43→      git_ref = "refs/pull/#{issue_number}/merge"
    44→
    45→      case get_project_for_qa(qa_result, repository_full_name) do
    46→        {:ok, project} ->
    47→          {_, prompt} = qa_result
    48→          test_qa_prompt(project, prompt, git_ref, repository_full_name)
    49→
    50→        {:error, :not_found} ->
    51→          case qa_result do
    52→            {project_name, _} when not is_nil(project_name) ->
    53→              VCS.create_comment(%{
    54→                repository_full_handle: repository_full_name,
    55→                git_ref: git_ref,
    56→                body: "Project '#{project_name}' is not connected to this repository.",
    57→                project: nil
    58→              })
    59→
    60→            _ ->
    61→              :ok
    62→          end
    63→
    64→        {:error, {:multiple_projects, projects}} ->
    65→          project_handles = Enum.map_join(projects, ", ", & &1.name)
    66→
    67→          VCS.create_comment(%{
    68→            repository_full_handle: repository_full_name,
    69→            git_ref: git_ref,
    70→            body:
    71→              "Multiple Tuist projects are connected to this repository. Please specify the project handle: `/tuist <project-handle> qa <your-prompt>`\n\nAvailable projects: #{project_handles}",
    72→            project: List.first(projects)
    73→          })
    74→      end
    75→    end
    76→
    77→    conn
    78→    |> put_status(:ok)
    79→    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    80→  end
    81→
    82→  defp handle_issue_comment(conn, _params) do
    83→    conn
    84→    |> put_status(:ok)
    85→    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    86→  end
    87→
    88→  defp handle_installation(conn, %{"action" => "deleted", "installation" => %{"id" => installation_id}}) do
    89→    {:ok, _} = delete_github_app_installation(installation_id)
    90→
    91→    conn
    92→    |> put_status(:ok)
    93→    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    94→  end
    95→
    96→  defp handle_installation(conn, %{
    97→         "action" => "created",
    98→         "installation" => %{"id" => installation_id, "html_url" => html_url}
    99→       }) do
   100→    case update_github_app_installation_html_url_with_retry(installation_id, html_url) do
   101→      {:ok, _} ->
   102→        conn
   103→        |> put_status(:ok)
   104→        |> json(%{status: "ok"})
   105→
   106→      {:error, :not_found_after_retries} ->
   107→        # After retries, the installation still doesn't exist. This indicates a broken user flow:
   108→        # 1. The setup callback failed or was never called
   109→        # 2. The user closed the browser before completing setup
   110→        # 3. Network issues prevented the redirect
   111→        # This means the installation exists in GitHub but not in our database,
   112→        # creating an orphaned installation that requires manual reconciliation.
   113→        Logger.error(
   114→          "GitHub installation.created webhook for installation_id=#{installation_id} but installation not found after retries. Setup callback may have failed. Manual intervention may be required."
   115→        )
   116→
   117→        conn
   118→        |> put_status(:ok)
   119→        |> json(%{status: "ok"})
   120→    end
   121→  end
   122→
   123→  defp handle_installation(conn, _params) do
   124→    conn
   125→    |> put_status(:ok)
   126→    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
   127→  end
   128→
   129→  defp get_project_for_qa(qa_result, repository_full_name) do
   130→    case qa_result do
   131→      {project_name, _prompt} when not is_nil(project_name) ->
   132→        Projects.project_by_name_and_vcs_repository_full_handle(
   133→          project_name,
   134→          repository_full_name,
   135→          preload: :account
   136→        )
   137→
   138→      {nil, _prompt} ->
   139→        projects =
   140→          Projects.projects_by_vcs_repository_full_handle(repository_full_name, preload: :account)
   141→
   142→        case projects do
   143→          [] -> {:error, :not_found}
   144→          [project] -> {:ok, project}
   145→          multiple -> {:error, {:multiple_projects, multiple}}
   146→        end
   147→    end
   148→  end
   149→
   150→  defp test_qa_prompt(project, prompt, git_ref, repository_full_handle) do
   151→    if FunWithFlags.enabled?(:qa, for: project.account) do
   152→      start_or_enqueue_qa_run(project, prompt, git_ref)
   153→    else
   154→      VCS.create_comment(%{
   155→        repository_full_handle: repository_full_handle,
   156→        git_ref: git_ref,
   157→        body:
   158→          "Tuist QA is currently not generally available. Contact us at contact@tuist.dev if you'd like an early preview of the feature.",
   159→        project: project
   160→      })
   161→    end
   162→  end
   163→
   164→  defp start_or_enqueue_qa_run(project, prompt, git_ref) do
   165→    project = Repo.preload(project, :vcs_connection)
   166→
   167→    simulator_app_build =
   168→      AppBuilds.latest_app_build(git_ref, project, supported_platform: :ios_simulator)
   169→
   170→    {:ok, qa_run} =
   171→      QA.create_qa_run(%{
   172→        app_build_id: simulator_app_build && simulator_app_build.id,
   173→        prompt: prompt,
   174→        status: "pending",
   175→        git_ref: git_ref,
   176→        issue_comment_id: nil
   177→      })
   178→
   179→    {:ok, %{"id" => comment_id}} =
   180→      post_initial_comment(
   181→        simulator_app_build,
   182→        project.vcs_connection && project.vcs_connection.repository_full_handle,
   183→        git_ref,
   184→        project,
   185→        qa_run
   186→      )
   187→
   188→    {:ok, updated_qa_run} = QA.update_qa_run(qa_run, %{issue_comment_id: comment_id})
   189→
   190→    if simulator_app_build do
   191→      QA.enqueue_test_worker(updated_qa_run)
   192→    end
   193→  end
   194→
   195→  defp post_initial_comment(simulator_app_build, repository_full_handle, git_ref, project, qa_run) do
   196→    qa_run_url =
   197→      "#{Environment.app_url()}/#{project.account.name}/#{project.name}/qa/#{qa_run.id}"
   198→
   199→    body =
   200→      if simulator_app_build do
   201→        simulator_app_build = Repo.preload(simulator_app_build, :preview)
   202→        preview = simulator_app_build.preview
   203→
   204→        preview_url =
   205→          "#{Environment.app_url()}/#{project.account.name}/#{project.name}/previews/#{preview.id}"
   206→
   207→        "Running QA for [#{preview.display_name}](#{preview_url}). [See the live QA session](#{qa_run_url})."
   208→      else
   209→        "No preview found for your PR. Tuist QA will be triggered as soon as a Tuist Preview is available. Make sure to include `tuist share` as part of your CI pipeline. For more details on how to set up Previews, head over to our [documentation](https://docs.tuist.dev/en/guides/features/previews)."
   210→      end
   211→
   212→    VCS.create_comment(%{
   213→      repository_full_handle: repository_full_handle,
   214→      git_ref: git_ref,
   215→      body: body,
   216→      project: project
   217→    })
   218→  end
   219→
   220→  defp tuist_qa_prompt(body) do
   221→    base_command =
   222→      cond do
   223→        Environment.stag?() -> "/tuist-staging"
   224→        Environment.dev?() -> "/tuist-development"
   225→        Environment.can?() -> "/tuist-canary"
   226→        true -> "/tuist"
   227→      end
   228→
   229→    # Pattern 1: /tuist <project-name> qa <prompt> (with project name)
   230→    pattern_with_project = ~r/^\s*#{Regex.escape(base_command)}\s+(\S+)\s+qa\s+(.*)$/im
   231→
   232→    # Pattern 2: /tuist qa <prompt> (no project name, valid in case only a single Tuist project is connected to the GitHub repository)
   233→    pattern_without_project = ~r/^\s*#{Regex.escape(base_command)}\s+qa\s+(.*)$/im
   234→
   235→    case Regex.run(pattern_with_project, body) do
   236→      [_, project_name, prompt] ->
   237→        {String.trim(project_name), String.trim(prompt)}
   238→
   239→      _ ->
   240→        case Regex.run(pattern_without_project, body) do
   241→          [_, prompt] ->
   242→            {nil, String.trim(prompt)}
   243→
   244→          _ ->
   245→            nil
   246→        end
   247→    end
   248→  end
   249→
   250→  defp delete_github_app_installation(installation_id) do
   251→    case VCS.get_github_app_installation_by_installation_id(installation_id) do
   252→      {:ok, github_app_installation} ->
   253→        VCS.delete_github_app_installation(github_app_installation)
   254→
   255→      {:error, :not_found} ->
   256→        {:ok, :already_deleted}
   257→    end
   258→  end
   259→
   260→  defp update_github_app_installation_html_url(installation_id, html_url) do
   261→    case VCS.get_github_app_installation_by_installation_id(installation_id) do
   262→      {:ok, github_app_installation} ->
   263→        VCS.update_github_app_installation(github_app_installation, %{html_url: html_url})
   264→
   265→      {:error, :not_found} ->
   266→        {:error, :not_found}
   267→    end
   268→  end
   269→
   270→  defp update_github_app_installation_html_url_with_retry(installation_id, html_url, attempt \\ 1) do
   271→    max_attempts = 3
   272→    retry_delay_ms = 1000
   273→
   274→    case update_github_app_installation_html_url(installation_id, html_url) do
   275→      {:ok, result} ->
   276→        {:ok, result}
   277→
   278→      {:error, :not_found} when attempt < max_attempts ->
   279→        Logger.info(
   280→          "GitHub installation not found for installation_id=#{installation_id}, attempt #{attempt}/#{max_attempts}. Retrying in #{retry_delay_ms}ms..."
   281→        )
   282→
   283→        Process.sleep(retry_delay_ms)
   284→        update_github_app_installation_html_url_with_retry(installation_id, html_url, attempt + 1)
   285→
   286→      {:error, :not_found} ->
   287→        {:error, :not_found_after_retries}
   288→    end
   289→  end
   290→end
   291→

Find the GitHub webhook endpoint handler in the server/ directory. I need to understand:
1. The route definition for /webhooks/github
2. The controller/plug that handles it
3. Any error handling already in place

Search for files related to GitHub webhooks in the server/ directory. Look at the router, controllers, and any related modules.

Find the GitHub webhook endpoint handler in the server/ directory. I need to understand:
1. The route definition for /webhooks/github
2. The controller/plug that handles it
3. Any error handling already in place

Search for files related to GitHub webhooks in the server/ directory. Look at the router, controllers, and any related modules.

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlugTest do
     2→  use ExUnit.Case, async: true
     3→
     4→  import Plug.Conn
     5→  import Plug.Test
     6→
     7→  alias TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug
     8→
     9→  defmodule TestHandler do
    10→    @moduledoc false
    11→    def handle(conn, payload) do
    12→      send(self(), {:handled, conn, payload})
    13→      conn
    14→    end
    15→  end
    16→
    17→  defmodule TimeoutBodyReader do
    18→    @moduledoc false
    19→
    20→    def read_body(_conn, _opts) do
    21→      raise Bandit.HTTPError, message: "Body read timeout", plug_status: :request_timeout
    22→    end
    23→  end
    24→
    25→  defp generate_signature(payload, secret, prefix \\ nil) do
    26→    signature = :hmac |> :crypto.mac(:sha256, secret, payload) |> Base.encode16(case: :lower)
    27→
    28→    if prefix do
    29→      "#{prefix}#{signature}"
    30→    else
    31→      signature
    32→    end
    33→  end
    34→
    35→  defp webhook_request(method, path, payload, secret, header_name, prefix \\ nil) do
    36→    signature = generate_signature(payload, secret, prefix)
    37→
    38→    method
    39→    |> conn(path, payload)
    40→    |> put_req_header("content-type", "application/json")
    41→    |> put_req_header(header_name, signature)
    42→    |> Map.update!(:body_params, fn _ -> Jason.decode!(payload) end)
    43→    |> assign(:raw_body, [payload])
    44→  end
    45→
    46→  describe "call/2 with GitHub-style signature (with sha256= prefix)" do
    47→    test "processes webhook at configured path with valid signature" do
    48→      # Given
    49→      secret = "my-secret"
    50→      payload = ~s({"action": "opened"})
    51→
    52→      options = [
    53→        at: "/webhook/github",
    54→        secret: secret,
    55→        handler: TestHandler,
    56→        signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
    57→        signature_prefix: "sha256="
    58→      ]
    59→
    60→      conn = webhook_request(:post, "/webhook/github", payload, secret, "x-hub-signature-256", "sha256=")
    61→
    62→      # When
    63→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
    64→
    65→      # Then
    66→      assert result.status == 200
    67→      assert result.resp_body == "OK"
    68→      assert result.halted == true
    69→
    70→      assert_receive {:handled, _conn, %{"action" => "opened"}}
    71→    end
    72→
    73→    test "returns 403 for invalid signature" do
    74→      # Given
    75→      secret = "my-secret"
    76→      invalid_secret = "wrong-secret"
    77→      payload = ~s({"action": "opened"})
    78→
    79→      options = [
    80→        at: "/webhook/github",
    81→        secret: secret,
    82→        handler: TestHandler,
    83→        signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
    84→        signature_prefix: "sha256="
    85→      ]
    86→
    87→      conn = webhook_request(:post, "/webhook/github", payload, invalid_secret, "x-hub-signature-256", "sha256=")
    88→
    89→      # When
    90→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
    91→
    92→      # Then
    93→      assert result.status == 403
    94→      assert result.resp_body == "Invalid signature"
    95→      assert result.halted == true
    96→
    97→      refute_receive {:handled, _, _}
    98→    end
    99→
   100→    test "rejects tampered payload" do
   101→      # Given
   102→      secret = "test-secret"
   103→      original_payload = ~s({"action": "opened"})
   104→      tampered_payload = ~s({"action": "closed"})
   105→
   106→      options = [
   107→        at: "/webhook",
   108→        secret: secret,
   109→        handler: TestHandler,
   110→        signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
   111→        signature_prefix: "sha256="
   112→      ]
   113→
   114→      signature = generate_signature(original_payload, secret, "sha256=")
   115→
   116→      conn =
   117→        :post
   118→        |> conn("/webhook", tampered_payload)
   119→        |> put_req_header("content-type", "application/json")
   120→        |> put_req_header("x-hub-signature-256", signature)
   121→        |> Map.update!(:body_params, fn _ -> Jason.decode!(tampered_payload) end)
   122→        |> assign(:raw_body, [tampered_payload])
   123→
   124→      # When
   125→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
   126→
   127→      # Then
   128→      assert result.status == 403
   129→    end
   130→
   131→    test "processes payload split across multiple body reads" do
   132→      # Given
   133→      secret = "my-secret"
   134→      payload = ~s({"action": "opened", "body": "chunked-payload"})
   135→
   136→      parser_opts =
   137→        Plug.Parsers.init(
   138→          parsers: [:json],
   139→          body_reader: {WebhookPlug.CacheBodyReader, :read_body, []},
   140→          json_decoder: Phoenix.json_library(),
   141→          read_length: 8
   142→        )
   143→
   144→      options = [
   145→        at: "/webhook/github",
   146→        secret: secret,
   147→        handler: TestHandler,
   148→        signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
   149→        signature_prefix: "sha256=",
   150→        parser_opts: parser_opts
   151→      ]
   152→
   153→      signature = generate_signature(payload, secret, "sha256=")
   154→
   155→      conn =
   156→        :post
   157→        |> conn("/webhook/github", payload)
   158→        |> put_req_header("content-type", "application/json")
   159→        |> put_req_header("x-hub-signature-256", signature)
   160→
   161→      # When
   162→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, options)
   163→
   164→      # Then
   165→      assert result.status == 200
   166→      assert result.resp_body == "OK"
   167→      assert result.halted == true
   168→
   169→      assert_receive {:handled, _conn, %{"action" => "opened", "body" => "chunked-payload"}}
   170→    end
   171→
   172→    test "returns 408 when the body read times out" do
   173→      # Given
   174→      secret = "my-secret"
   175→      payload = ~s({"action": "opened"})
   176→
   177→      parser_opts =
   178→        Plug.Parsers.init(
   179→          parsers: [:json],
   180→          body_reader: {TimeoutBodyReader, :read_body, []},
   181→          json_decoder: Phoenix.json_library()
   182→        )
   183→
   184→      options = [
   185→        at: "/webhook/github",
   186→        secret: secret,
   187→        handler: TestHandler,
   188→        signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
   189→        signature_prefix: "sha256=",
   190→        parser_opts: parser_opts
   191→      ]
   192→
   193→      signature = generate_signature(payload, secret, "sha256=")
   194→
   195→      conn =
   196→        :post
   197→        |> conn("/webhook/github", payload)
   198→        |> put_req_header("content-type", "application/json")
   199→        |> put_req_header("x-hub-signature-256", signature)
   200→
   201→      # When
   202→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, options)
   203→
   204→      # Then
   205→      assert result.status == 408
   206→      assert result.resp_body == "Request Timeout"
   207→      assert result.halted == true
   208→
   209→      refute_receive {:handled, _conn, _payload}
   210→    end
   211→  end
   212→
   213→  describe "call/2 with cache-style signature (no prefix)" do
   214→    test "processes webhook at configured path with valid signature" do
   215→      # Given
   216→      secret = "my-secret"
   217→      payload = ~s({"events": [{"action": "upload", "size": 1024, "cas_id": "abc123"}]})
   218→
   219→      options = [
   220→        at: "/webhooks/cache",
   221→        secret: secret,
   222→        handler: TestHandler,
   223→        signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
   224→      ]
   225→
   226→      conn = webhook_request(:post, "/webhooks/cache", payload, secret, "x-cache-signature")
   227→
   228→      # When
   229→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
   230→
   231→      # Then
   232→      assert result.status == 200
   233→      assert result.resp_body == "OK"
   234→      assert result.halted == true
   235→
   236→      assert_receive {:handled, _conn, %{"events" => [%{"action" => "upload", "size" => 1024, "cas_id" => "abc123"}]}}
   237→    end
   238→
   239→    test "returns 403 for invalid signature" do
   240→      # Given
   241→      secret = "my-secret"
   242→      invalid_secret = "wrong-secret"
   243→      payload = ~s({"events": []})
   244→
   245→      options = [
   246→        at: "/webhooks/cache",
   247→        secret: secret,
   248→        handler: TestHandler,
   249→        signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
   250→      ]
   251→
   252→      conn = webhook_request(:post, "/webhooks/cache", payload, invalid_secret, "x-cache-signature")
   253→
   254→      # When
   255→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
   256→
   257→      # Then
   258→      assert result.status == 403
   259→      assert result.resp_body == "Invalid signature"
   260→      assert result.halted == true
   261→
   262→      refute_receive {:handled, _, _}
   263→    end
   264→
   265→    test "returns 401 when signature header is missing" do
   266→      # Given
   267→      secret = "my-secret"
   268→      payload = ~s({"events": []})
   269→
   270→      options = [
   271→        at: "/webhooks/cache",
   272→        secret: secret,
   273→        handler: TestHandler,
   274→        signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
   275→      ]
   276→
   277→      conn =
   278→        :post
   279→        |> conn("/webhooks/cache", payload)
   280→        |> put_req_header("content-type", "application/json")
   281→        |> Map.update!(:body_params, fn _ -> Jason.decode!(payload) end)
   282→        |> assign(:raw_body, [payload])
   283→
   284→      # When
   285→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
   286→
   287→      # Then
   288→      assert result.status == 401
   289→      assert result.resp_body == "Missing x-cache-signature header"
   290→      assert result.halted == true
   291→
   292→      refute_receive {:handled, _, _}
   293→    end
   294→
   295→    test "rejects tampered payload" do
   296→      # Given
   297→      secret = "test-secret"
   298→      original_payload = ~s({"events": [{"action": "upload"}]})
   299→      tampered_payload = ~s({"events": [{"action": "download"}]})
   300→
   301→      options = [
   302→        at: "/webhooks/cache",
   303→        secret: secret,
   304→        handler: TestHandler,
   305→        signature_header: "x-cache-signature"
   306→      ]
   307→
   308→      signature = generate_signature(original_payload, secret)
   309→
   310→      conn =
   311→        :post
   312→        |> conn("/webhooks/cache", tampered_payload)
   313→        |> put_req_header("content-type", "application/json")
   314→        |> put_req_header("x-cache-signature", signature)
   315→        |> Map.update!(:body_params, fn _ -> Jason.decode!(tampered_payload) end)
   316→        |> assign(:raw_body, [tampered_payload])
   317→
   318→      # When
   319→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
   320→
   321→      # Then
   322→      assert result.status == 403
   323→      assert result.resp_body == "Invalid signature"
   324→      assert result.halted == true
   325→
   326→      refute_receive {:handled, _, _}
   327→    end
   328→  end
   329→
   330→  describe "call/2 for non-matching paths" do
   331→    test "passes through for non-matching paths" do
   332→      # Given
   333→      secret = "my-secret"
   334→      payload = ~s({"action": "opened"})
   335→
   336→      options = [
   337→        at: "/webhook/github",
   338→        secret: secret,
   339→        handler: TestHandler,
   340→        signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256"
   341→      ]
   342→
   343→      conn = webhook_request(:post, "/other/path", payload, secret, "x-hub-signature-256")
   344→
   345→      # When
   346→      result = WebhookPlug.call(conn, WebhookPlug.init(options))
   347→
   348→      # Then
   349→      refute result.halted
   350→      refute result.status
   351→      refute_receive {:handled, _, _}
   352→    end
   353→  end
   354→end

Perfect! I now have a comprehensive understanding of the GitHub webhook endpoint handler. Let me compile the findings:

## GitHub Webhook Endpoint Handler Summary

I found the complete GitHub webhook implementation in the Tuist Server. Here's what you need to know:

### 1. Route Definition for /webhooks/github

**File:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/endpoint.ex` (lines 66-72)

```elixir
plug WebhookPlug,
  at: "/webhooks/github",
  handler: GitHubController,
  secret: {Tuist.Environment, :github_app_webhook_secret, []},
  signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
  signature_prefix: "sha256=",
  read_timeout: 60_000
```

The endpoint is configured with:
- **Path:** `/webhooks/github`
- **Handler:** `TuistWeb.Webhooks.GitHubController`
- **Secret:** Retrieved from `Tuist.Environment.github_app_webhook_secret()`
- **Signature Header:** `x-hub-signature-256` (GitHub's standard)
- **Signature Prefix:** `sha256=` (GitHub uses this prefix format)
- **Read Timeout:** 60 seconds (custom, longer than the default 15 seconds)

### 2. Controller/Plug Implementation

**Plug:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/plugs/webhook_plug.ex`

This is a generic webhook handler plug that:
- Verifies HMAC-SHA256 signatures using `Plug.Crypto.secure_compare`
- Uses a custom body reader (`CacheBodyReader`) to cache raw body content for signature verification
- Handles request timeouts (408 response)
- Requires matching signature headers (401 if missing, 403 if invalid)
- Passes verified requests to the handler module

**Handler:** `/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3/server/lib/tuist_web/controllers/webhooks/github_controller.ex`

The GitHubController handles two event types:

1. **`issue_comment`** events (lines 30-80):
   - Triggers on comment creation in pull requests
   - Parses `/tuist` QA commands from comment body
   - Supports two formats:
     - `/tuist <project-name> qa <prompt>` (explicit project)
     - `/tuist qa <prompt>` (auto-detect single project)
   - Creates QA runs and posts status comments back to GitHub

2. **`installation`** events (lines 88-127):
   - **`created`:** Updates GitHub app installation HTML URL with retry logic
   - **`deleted`:** Removes GitHub app installation from database
   - Returns OK for other actions

### 3. Error Handling Already in Place

**Signature Verification Errors (WebhookPlug):**
- **401 Unauthorized:** Missing signature header with custom message showing which header is expected
- **403 Forbidden:** Invalid signature (tampered payload)
- **408 Request Timeout:** Body read timeout (configurable, 60s for GitHub webhooks)

**Request Processing Errors (GitHubController):**
- **Project Not Found:** Posts GitHub comment saying "Project 'X' is not connected to this repository"
- **Multiple Projects:** Posts GitHub comment asking user to specify project name and lists available projects
- **QA Feature Disabled:** Posts GitHub comment explaining QA is not available
- **Installation Not Found After Retries:** Logs error level warning and returns 200 OK (graceful degradation)

**Response Status:**
- Always returns **200 OK** from the controller (even for unhandled event types) to acknowledge receipt
- If the controller doesn't set a status, the plug automatically sends 200 OK with "OK" body

The implementation is quite robust with retry logic for installation creation (up to 3 attempts with 1-second delays) and comprehensive test coverage for all error scenarios.

defmodule TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug do
  @moduledoc """
  Generic webhook handler with HMAC signature verification.

  Supports different signature formats and headers through configuration.
  """

  @behaviour Plug

  import Plug.Conn

  require Logger

  defmodule CacheBodyReader do
    @moduledoc false

    def read_body(conn, opts) do
      case Plug.Conn.read_body(conn, opts) do
        {:ok, body, conn} ->
          {:ok, body, cache_raw_body(conn, body)}

        {:more, body, conn} ->
          {:more, body, cache_raw_body(conn, body)}

        {:error, reason} ->
          {:error, reason}
      end
    end

    defp cache_raw_body(conn, body) do
      update_in(conn.assigns[:raw_body], fn
        nil -> body
        existing -> [existing, body]
      end)
    end
  end

  @impl true
  def init(options) do
    read_timeout = Keyword.get(options, :read_timeout, 15_000)

    parser_opts =
      Plug.Parsers.init(
        parsers: [:json],
        body_reader: {CacheBodyReader, :read_body, []},
        json_decoder: Phoenix.json_library(),
        read_timeout: read_timeout
      )

    Keyword.put(options, :parser_opts, parser_opts)
  end

  @doc """
  Verifies HMAC signature and calls a handler with the webhook payload
  """
  @impl true
  def call(conn, options) do
    path = get_config(options, :at)

    case conn.request_path do
      ^path ->
        handle_webhook(conn, options)

      _ ->
        conn
    end
  end

  defp handle_webhook(conn, options) do
    secret = parse_secret!(get_config(options, :secret))
    module = get_config(options, :handler)
    signature_header = get_config(options, :signature_header) || "x-hub-signature-256"
    signature_prefix = get_config(options, :signature_prefix)
    parser_opts = get_config(options, :parser_opts)

    conn =
      try do
        Plug.Parsers.call(conn, parser_opts)
      rescue
        error in Bandit.HTTPError ->
          if error.plug_status == :request_timeout do
            conn
            |> send_resp(408, "Request Timeout")
            |> halt()
          else
            reraise error, __STACKTRACE__
          end
      end

    if conn.halted do
      conn
    else
      signature = conn |> get_req_header(signature_header) |> List.first()

      cond do
        is_nil(signature) ->
          conn
          |> send_resp(401, "Missing #{signature_header} header")
          |> halt()

        conn.assigns.raw_body
        |> List.wrap()
        |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
        |> verify_signature(
          secret,
          signature,
          signature_prefix
        ) ->
          handle_verified_webhook(conn, module)

        true ->
          conn
          |> send_resp(403, "Invalid signature")
          |> halt()
      end
    end
  end

  defp handle_verified_webhook(conn, module) do
    result_conn = module.handle(conn, conn.body_params)

    if result_conn.status do
      result_conn
    else
      result_conn |> send_resp(200, "OK") |> halt()
    end
  end

  defp verify_signature(payload, secret, signature_in_header, signature_prefix) do
    expected_signature =
      :hmac
      |> :crypto.mac(:sha256, secret, payload)
      |> Base.encode16(case: :lower)

    expected_signature_with_prefix =
      if signature_prefix do
        signature_prefix <> expected_signature
      else
        expected_signature
      end

    Plug.Crypto.secure_compare(signature_in_header, expected_signature_with_prefix)
  end

  defp parse_secret!({m, f, a}), do: apply(m, f, a)
  defp parse_secret!(fun) when is_function(fun), do: fun.()
  defp parse_secret!(secret) when is_binary(secret), do: secret

  defp parse_secret!(secret) do
    raise """
    The webhook secret is invalid. Expected a string, tuple, or function.
    Got: #{inspect(secret)}

    If you're setting the secret at runtime, you need to pass a tuple or function.
    For example:

    plug TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug,
      at: "/webhook/example",
      handler: MyHandler,
      secret: {Application, :get_env, [:myapp, :webhook_secret]},
      signature_header: "x-signature",
      signature_prefix: "sha256="
    """
  end

  defp get_config(options, key) do
    options[key]
  end
end

1→defmodule TuistWeb.Webhooks.GitHubController do
     2→  use TuistWeb, :controller
     3→
     4→  alias Tuist.AppBuilds
     5→  alias Tuist.Environment
     6→  alias Tuist.Projects
     7→  alias Tuist.QA
     8→  alias Tuist.Repo
     9→  alias Tuist.VCS
    10→
    11→  require Logger
    12→
    13→  def handle(conn, params) do
    14→    event_type = conn |> get_req_header("x-github-event") |> List.first()
    15→
    16→    case event_type do
    17→      "issue_comment" ->
    18→        handle_issue_comment(conn, params)
    19→
    20→      "installation" ->
    21→        handle_installation(conn, params)
    22→
    23→      _ ->
    24→        conn
    25→        |> put_status(:ok)
    26→        |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    27→    end
    28→  end
    29→
    30→  defp handle_issue_comment(
    31→         conn,
    32→         %{
    33→           "action" => "created",
    34→           "comment" => %{"body" => comment_body},
    35→           "repository" => repository,
    36→           "issue" => %{"number" => issue_number, "pull_request" => _pull_request}
    37→         } = _params
    38→       ) do
    39→    qa_result = tuist_qa_prompt(comment_body)
    40→
    41→    if qa_result do
    42→      repository_full_name = repository["full_name"]
    43→      git_ref = "refs/pull/#{issue_number}/merge"
    44→
    45→      case get_project_for_qa(qa_result, repository_full_name) do
    46→        {:ok, project} ->
    47→          {_, prompt} = qa_result
    48→          test_qa_prompt(project, prompt, git_ref, repository_full_name)
    49→
    50→        {:error, :not_found} ->
    51→          case qa_result do
    52→            {project_name, _} when not is_nil(project_name) ->
    53→              VCS.create_comment(%{
    54→                repository_full_handle: repository_full_name,
    55→                git_ref: git_ref,
    56→                body: "Project '#{project_name}' is not connected to this repository.",
    57→                project: nil
    58→              })
    59→
    60→            _ ->
    61→              :ok
    62→          end
    63→
    64→        {:error, {:multiple_projects, projects}} ->
    65→          project_handles = Enum.map_join(projects, ", ", & &1.name)
    66→
    67→          VCS.create_comment(%{
    68→            repository_full_handle: repository_full_name,
    69→            git_ref: git_ref,
    70→            body:
    71→              "Multiple Tuist projects are connected to this repository. Please specify the project handle: `/tuist <project-handle> qa <your-prompt>`\n\nAvailable projects: #{project_handles}",
    72→            project: List.first(projects)
    73→          })
    74→      end
    75→    end
    76→
    77→    conn
    78→    |> put_status(:ok)
    79→    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    80→  end
    81→
    82→  defp handle_issue_comment(conn, _params) do
    83→    conn
    84→    |> put_status(:ok)
    85→    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    86→  end
    87→
    88→  defp handle_installation(conn, %{"action" => "deleted", "installation" => %{"id" => installation_id}}) do
    89→    {:ok, _} = delete_github_app_installation(installation_id)
    90→
    91→    conn
    92→    |> put_status(:ok)
    93→    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    94→  end
    95→
    96→  defp handle_installation(conn, %{
    97→         "action" => "created",
    98→         "installation" => %{"id" => installation_id, "html_url" => html_url}
    99→       }) do
   100→    case update_github_app_installation_html_url_with_retry(installation_id, html_url) do
   101→      {:ok, _} ->
   102→        conn
   103→        |> put_status(:ok)
   104→        |> json(%{status: "ok"})

defmodule TuistWeb.Webhooks.GitHubController do
  use TuistWeb, :controller

  alias Tuist.AppBuilds
  alias Tuist.Environment
  alias Tuist.Projects
  alias Tuist.QA
  alias Tuist.Repo
  alias Tuist.VCS

  require Logger

  def handle(conn, params) do
    event_type = conn |> get_req_header("x-github-event") |> List.first()

    case event_type do
      "issue_comment" ->
        handle_issue_comment(conn, params)

      "installation" ->
        handle_installation(conn, params)

      _ ->
        conn
        |> put_status(:ok)
        |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    end
  end

  defp handle_issue_comment(
         conn,
         %{
           "action" => "created",
           "comment" => %{"body" => comment_body},
           "repository" => repository,
           "issue" => %{"number" => issue_number, "pull_request" => _pull_request}
         } = _params
       ) do
    qa_result = tuist_qa_prompt(comment_body)

    if qa_result do
      repository_full_name = repository["full_name"]
      git_ref = "refs/pull/#{issue_number}/merge"

      case get_project_for_qa(qa_result, repository_full_name) do
        {:ok, project} ->
          {_, prompt} = qa_result
          test_qa_prompt(project, prompt, git_ref, repository_full_name)

        {:error, :not_found} ->
          case qa_result do
            {project_name, _} when not is_nil(project_name) ->
              VCS.create_comment(%{
                repository_full_handle: repository_full_name,
                git_ref: git_ref,
                body: "Project '#{project_name}' is not connected to this repository.",
                project: nil
              })

            _ ->
              :ok
          end

        {:error, {:multiple_projects, projects}} ->
          project_handles = Enum.map_join(projects, ", ", & &1.name)

          VCS.create_comment(%{
            repository_full_handle: repository_full_name,
            git_ref: git_ref,
            body:
              "Multiple Tuist projects are connected to this repository. Please specify the project handle: `/tuist <project-handle> qa <your-prompt>`\n\nAvailable projects: #{project_handles}",
            project: List.first(projects)
          })
      end
    end

    conn
    |> put_status(:ok)
    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
  end

  defp handle_issue_comment(conn, _params) do
    conn
    |> put_status(:ok)
    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
  end

  defp handle_installation(conn, %{"action" => "deleted", "installation" => %{"id" => installation_id}}) do
    {:ok, _} = delete_github_app_installation(installation_id)

    conn
    |> put_status(:ok)
    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
  end

  defp handle_installation(conn, %{
         "action" => "created",
         "installation" => %{"id" => installation_id, "html_url" => html_url}
       }) do
    case update_github_app_installation_html_url_with_retry(installation_id, html_url) do
      {:ok, _} ->
        conn
        |> put_status(:ok)
        |> json(%{status: "ok"})

      {:error, :not_found_after_retries} ->
        # After retries, the installation still doesn't exist. This indicates a broken user flow:
        # 1. The setup callback failed or was never called
        # 2. The user closed the browser before completing setup
        # 3. Network issues prevented the redirect
        # This means the installation exists in GitHub but not in our database,
        # creating an orphaned installation that requires manual reconciliation.
        Logger.error(
          "GitHub installation.created webhook for installation_id=#{installation_id} but installation not found after retries. Setup callback may have failed. Manual intervention may be required."
        )

        conn
        |> put_status(:ok)
        |> json(%{status: "ok"})
    end
  end

  defp handle_installation(conn, _params) do
    conn
    |> put_status(:ok)
    |> json(%{status: "ok"})
  end

  defp get_project_for_qa(qa_result, repository_full_name) do
    case qa_result do
      {project_name, _prompt} when not is_nil(project_name) ->
        Projects.project_by_name_and_vcs_repository_full_handle(
          project_name,
          repository_full_name,
          preload: :account
        )

      {nil, _prompt} ->
        projects =
          Projects.projects_by_vcs_repository_full_handle(repository_full_name, preload: :account)

        case projects do
          [] -> {:error, :not_found}
          [project] -> {:ok, project}
          multiple -> {:error, {:multiple_projects, multiple}}
        end
    end
  end

  defp test_qa_prompt(project, prompt, git_ref, repository_full_handle) do
    if FunWithFlags.enabled?(:qa, for: project.account) do
      start_or_enqueue_qa_run(project, prompt, git_ref)
    else
      VCS.create_comment(%{
        repository_full_handle: repository_full_handle,
        git_ref: git_ref,
        body:
          "Tuist QA is currently not generally available. Contact us at contact@tuist.dev if you'd like an early preview of the feature.",
        project: project
      })
    end
  end

  defp start_or_enqueue_qa_run(project, prompt, git_ref) do
    project = Repo.preload(project, :vcs_connection)

    simulator_app_build =
      AppBuilds.latest_app_build(git_ref, project, supported_platform: :ios_simulator)

    {:ok, qa_run} =
      QA.create_qa_run(%{
        app_build_id: simulator_app_build && simulator_app_build.id,
        prompt: prompt,
        status: "pending",
        git_ref: git_ref,
        issue_comment_id: nil
      })

    {:ok, %{"id" => comment_id}} =
      post_initial_comment(
        simulator_app_build,
        project.vcs_connection && project.vcs_connection.repository_full_handle,
        git_ref,
        project,
        qa_run
      )

    {:ok, updated_qa_run} = QA.update_qa_run(qa_run, %{issue_comment_id: comment_id})

    if simulator_app_build do
      QA.enqueue_test_worker(updated_qa_run)
    end
  end

  defp post_initial_comment(simulator_app_build, repository_full_handle, git_ref, project, qa_run) do
    qa_run_url =
      "#{Environment.app_url()}/#{project.account.name}/#{project.name}/qa/#{qa_run.id}"

    body =
      if simulator_app_build do
        simulator_app_build = Repo.preload(simulator_app_build, :preview)
        preview = simulator_app_build.preview

        preview_url =
          "#{Environment.app_url()}/#{project.account.name}/#{project.name}/previews/#{preview.id}"

        "Running QA for [#{preview.display_name}](#{preview_url}). [See the live QA session](#{qa_run_url})."
      else
        "No preview found for your PR. Tuist QA will be triggered as soon as a Tuist Preview is available. Make sure to include `tuist share` as part of your CI pipeline. For more details on how to set up Previews, head over to our [documentation](https://docs.tuist.dev/en/guides/features/previews)."
      end

    VCS.create_comment(%{
      repository_full_handle: repository_full_handle,
      git_ref: git_ref,
      body: body,
      project: project
    })
  end

  defp tuist_qa_prompt(body) do
    base_command =
      cond do
        Environment.stag?() -> "/tuist-staging"
        Environment.dev?() -> "/tuist-development"
        Environment.can?() -> "/tuist-canary"
        true -> "/tuist"
      end

    # Pattern 1: /tuist <project-name> qa <prompt> (with project name)
    pattern_with_project = ~r/^\s*#{Regex.escape(base_command)}\s+(\S+)\s+qa\s+(.*)$/im

    # Pattern 2: /tuist qa <prompt> (no project name, valid in case only a single Tuist project is connected to the GitHub repository)
    pattern_without_project = ~r/^\s*#{Regex.escape(base_command)}\s+qa\s+(.*)$/im

    case Regex.run(pattern_with_project, body) do
      [_, project_name, prompt] ->
        {String.trim(project_name), String.trim(prompt)}

      _ ->
        case Regex.run(pattern_without_project, body) do
          [_, prompt] ->
            {nil, String.trim(prompt)}

          _ ->
            nil
        end
    end
  end

  defp delete_github_app_installation(installation_id) do
    case VCS.get_github_app_installation_by_installation_id(installation_id) do
      {:ok, github_app_installation} ->
        VCS.delete_github_app_installation(github_app_installation)

      {:error, :not_found} ->
        {:ok, :already_deleted}
    end
  end

  defp update_github_app_installation_html_url(installation_id, html_url) do
    case VCS.get_github_app_installation_by_installation_id(installation_id) do
      {:ok, github_app_installation} ->
        VCS.update_github_app_installation(github_app_installation, %{html_url: html_url})

      {:error, :not_found} ->
        {:error, :not_found}
    end
  end

  defp update_github_app_installation_html_url_with_retry(installation_id, html_url, attempt \\ 1) do
    max_attempts = 3
    retry_delay_ms = 1000

    case update_github_app_installation_html_url(installation_id, html_url) do
      {:ok, result} ->
        {:ok, result}

      {:error, :not_found} when attempt < max_attempts ->
        Logger.info(
          "GitHub installation not found for installation_id=#{installation_id}, attempt #{attempt}/#{max_attempts}. Retrying in #{retry_delay_ms}ms..."
        )

        Process.sleep(retry_delay_ms)
        update_github_app_installation_html_url_with_retry(installation_id, html_url, attempt + 1)

      {:error, :not_found} ->
        {:error, :not_found_after_retries}
    end
  end
end

defmodule TuistWeb.Endpoint do
  use Sentry.PlugCapture
  use Phoenix.Endpoint, otp_app: :tuist

  # The session will be stored in the cookie and signed,
  # this means its contents can be read but not tampered with.
  # Set :encryption_salt if you would also like to encrypt it.
  alias TuistWeb.Plugs.WebhookPlug
  alias TuistWeb.Webhooks.BillingController
  alias TuistWeb.Webhooks.GitHubController

  @session_options [
    store: :cookie,
    key: "_tuist_key",
    signing_salt: "tmgjS63H",
    same_site: "Lax"
  ]

  socket "/live", Phoenix.LiveView.Socket,
    websocket: [connect_info: [:user_agent, session: @session_options]],
    longpoll: [connect_info: [session: @session_options]]

  socket "/socket", TuistWeb.Socket,
    websocket: true,
    longpoll: false

  # Serve at "/" the static files from "priv/static" directory.
  #
  # You should set gzip to true if you are running phx.digest
  # when deploying your static files in production.
  plug Plug.Static,
    at: "/",
    from: :tuist,
    gzip: true,
    cache_control_for_etags: "public, max-age=31536000, immutable",
    only: TuistWeb.static_paths()

  if Code.ensure_loaded?(Tidewave) do
    plug Tidewave
  end

  # Code reloading can be explicitly enabled under the
  # :code_reloader configuration of your endpoint.
  if code_reloading? do
    socket "/phoenix/live_reload/socket", Phoenix.LiveReloader.Socket
    plug Phoenix.LiveReloader
    plug Phoenix.CodeReloader
    plug Phoenix.Ecto.CheckRepoStatus, otp_app: :tuist
  end

  plug Phoenix.LiveDashboard.RequestLogger,
    param_key: "request_logger",
    cookie_key: "request_logger"

  plug Plug.RequestId
  plug TuistCommon.OtelRequestIdPlug
  plug Plug.Telemetry, event_prefix: [:phoenix, :endpoint]
  plug Sentry.PlugContext
  plug TuistWeb.Plugs.CloseConnectionOnErrorPlug

  plug Stripe.WebhookPlug,
    at: "/webhooks/stripe",
    handler: BillingController,
    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :stripe_endpoint_secret, []}

  plug WebhookPlug,
    at: "/webhooks/github",
    handler: GitHubController,
    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :github_app_webhook_secret, []},
    signature_header: "x-hub-signature-256",
    signature_prefix: "sha256=",
    read_timeout: 60_000

  plug WebhookPlug,
    at: "/webhooks/cache",
    handler: TuistWeb.Webhooks.CacheController,
    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :cache_api_key, []},
    signature_header: "x-cache-signature"

  plug WebhookPlug,
    at: "/webhooks/gradle-cache",
    handler: TuistWeb.Webhooks.GradleCacheController,
    secret: {Tuist.Environment, :cache_api_key, []},
    signature_header: "x-cache-signature"

  # The /api/runs endpoint can receive large payloads (files, cacheable_tasks, cas_outputs)
  # for projects with thousands of files. 50MB should accommodate most projects.
  # TODO: Consider streaming large arrays instead of loading everything into memory.
  plug Plug.Parsers,
    parsers: [:urlencoded, :multipart, :json],
    pass: ["*/*"],
    json_decoder: Phoenix.json_library(),
    length: 50_000_000

  plug Plug.MethodOverride
  plug Plug.Head
  plug Plug.Session, @session_options
  plug TuistWeb.Router
end

Based on the search results, here's comprehensive information about setting up the Sentry MCP server with Claude Code:

## Quick Setup

The simplest way to add Sentry to Claude Code is using the CLI command:

```bash
claude mcp add --transport http sentry https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp
```

Then authenticate:

Use `/mcp` to authenticate with your Sentry account

## What It Does

The Sentry MCP Server connects your LLM client to Sentry using the Model Context Protocol (MCP), giving your AI tools direct access to issues, errors, projects, and Seer analysis. Sentry hosts and manages a remote MCP server with OAuth authentication, so there's nothing to install.

## Using It

Once set up, you can ask Claude Code questions like:

"What are the most common errors in the last 24 hours?"

Once authenticated, all 16+ tools become available in your client.

## Alternative Installation Methods

**As a Plugin:**
The plugin automatically configures the Sentry MCP server on install. No additional setup required beyond restarting Claude Code.

**For Self-Hosted Sentry:**
If you're running a self-hosted Sentry installation, use the STDIO transport instead.

## Key Features

The Sentry MCP Server provides seamless integration with Seer, Sentry's AI agent. Through MCP, you can trigger Seer analysis, get AI-generated fix recommendations, and monitor fix status — all from within your LLM client.

input.prompt:
Find the Claude Code MCP server configuration file. It's typically at one of these locations:
- ~/.claude/settings.json (may have mcpServers key)  
- ~/.claude/settings.local.json
- A project-level .mcp.json file
- ~/.claude.json

Check these locations and any other files that might contain MCP server configurations (look for "attio", "pencil", "crosspost", "qmd", or "guck" references since those MCP servers are already configured). Report the exact file path and its contents.

input.command:
python3 << 'EOF'
import json

with open('/Users/pepicrft/.claude.json', 'r') as f:
    data = json.load(f)

# Look for the tuist3 project specifically
project_path = "/Users/pepicrft/src/github.com/tuist/tuist3"
if 'projects' in data and project_path in data['projects']:
    config = data['projects'][project_path]
    print(f"Project config for tuist3:")
    print(json.dumps(config, indent=2))
else:
    print(f"tuist3 project not found in projects")
    # Check what projects exist
    if 'projects' in data:
        print("\nAvailable projects:")
        for proj in data['projects'].keys():
            print(f"  {proj}")
EOF

The Sentry MCP server is already configured in your `~/.claude.json`. It's set up as an HTTP transport pointing to `https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp`. However, the tools aren't appearing in my available tools, which likely means authentication hasn't been completed yet.

You'll need to authenticate with Sentry by running `/mcp` in Claude Code and authenticating the Sentry server through OAuth. Once that's done, I'll be able to query the issue directly.

In the meantime, could you paste the error message/title from that Sentry issue? Based on the code I've already read, I can see the webhook plug handles `Bandit.HTTPError` for timeouts, but common unhandled errors would be things like:
- `Plug.Parsers.ParseError` (malformed JSON from GitHub)
- `Jason.DecodeError` (invalid JSON)  
- `Plug.Parsers.UnsupportedMediaTypeError` (wrong content type)

If you can share the error title, I can implement the fix right away.

content.content:
1→import Config
     2→
     3→# config/runtime.exs is executed for all environments, including
     4→# during releases. It is executed after compilation and before the
     5→# system starts, so it is typically used to load production configuration
     6→# and secrets from environment variables or elsewhere. Do not define
     7→# any compile-time configuration in here, as it won't be applied.
     8→# The block below contains prod specific runtime configuration.
     9→
    10→# ## Using releases
    11→#
    12→# If you use `mix release`, you need to explicitly enable the server
    13→# by passing the TUIST_WEB=true when you start it:
    14→#
    15→#     TUIST_WEB=true bin/tuist start
    16→#
    17→# Alternatively, you can use `mix phx.gen.release` to generate a `bin/server`
    18→# script that automatically sets the env var above.
    19→if Tuist.Environment.web?() do
    20→  config :tuist, TuistWeb.Endpoint, server: true
    21→end
    22→
    23→env = Tuist.Environment.env()
    24→secrets = Tuist.Environment.decrypt_secrets()
    25→secret_key_base = Tuist.Environment.secret_key_base(secrets)
    26→
    27→if env != :test do
    28→  config :tuist, TuistWeb.Endpoint, secret_key_base: secret_key_base
    29→end
    30→
    31→if Enum.member?([:prod, :stag, :can], env) do
    32→  database_url =
    33→    Tuist.Environment.database_url(secrets) ||
    34→      raise """
    35→      environment variable DATABASE_URL is missing.
    36→      For example: ecto://USER:PASS@HOST/DATABASE
    37→      """
    38→
    39→  parsed_url = URI.parse(database_url)
    40→  [username, password] = String.split(parsed_url.userinfo, ":")
    41→
    42→  socket_opts =
    43→    if Tuist.Environment.use_ipv6?(secrets) in ~w(true 1),
    44→      do: [:inet6, {:keepalive, true}],
    45→      else: [{:keepalive, true}]
    46→
    47→  database_options = [
    48→    pool_size: Tuist.Environment.database_pool_size(secrets),
    49→    queue_target: Tuist.Environment.database_queue_target(secrets),
    50→    queue_interval: Tuist.Environment.database_queue_interval(secrets),
    51→    database: String.replace_prefix(parsed_url.path, "/", ""),
    52→    username: username,
    53→    password: password,
    54→    hostname: parsed_url.host,
    55→    port: parsed_url.port || 5432,
    56→    socket_options: socket_opts,
    57→    parameters: [
    58→      tcp_keepalives_idle: "60",
    59→      tcp_keepalives_interval: "30",
    60→      tcp_keepalives_count: "3"
    61→    ]
    62→  ]
    63→
    64→  database_options =
    65→    if Tuist.Environment.use_ssl_for_database?() do
    66→      Keyword.put(database_options, :ssl,
    67→        server_name_indication: to_charlist(parsed_url.host),
    68→        verify: :verify_none
    69→      )
    70→
    71→      # TODO: Add proper certificate verification
    72→    else
    73→      database_options
    74→    end
    75→
    76→  dns_name = System.get_env("RENDER_DISCOVERY_SERVICE")
    77→  app_name = System.get_env("RENDER_SERVICE_NAME")
    78→
    79→  config :libcluster,
    80→    topologies: [
    81→      render: [
    82→        strategy: Cluster.Strategy.Kubernetes.DNS,
    83→        config: [
    84→          service: dns_name,
    85→          application_name: app_name
    86→        ]
    87→      ]
    88→    ]
    89→
    90→  # ## SSL Support
    91→  #
    92→  # To get SSL working, you will need to add the `https` key
    93→  # to your endpoint configuration:
    94→  #
    95→  #     config :tuist, TuistWeb.Endpoint,
    96→  #       https: [
    97→  #         ...,
    98→  #         port: 443,
    99→  #         cipher_suite: :strong,
   100→  #         keyfile: System.get_env("SOME_APP_SSL_KEY_PATH"),
   101→  #         certfile: System.get_env("SOME_APP_SSL_CERT_PATH")
   102→  #       ]
   103→  #
   104→  # The `cipher_suite` is set to `:strong` to support only the
   105→  # latest and more secure SSL ciphers. This means old browsers
   106→  # and clients may not be supported. You can set it to
   107→  # `:compatible` for wider support.
   108→  #
   109→  # `:keyfile` and `:certfile` expect an absolute path to the key
   110→  # and cert in disk or a relative path inside priv, for example
   111→  # "priv/ssl/server.key". For all supported SSL configuration
   112→  # options, see https://hexdocs.pm/plug/Plug.SSL.html#configure/1
   113→  #
   114→  # We also recommend setting `force_ssl` in your config/prod.exs,
   115→  # ensuring no data is ever sent via http, always redirecting to https:
   116→  #
   117→  #     config :tuist, TuistWeb.Endpoint,
   118→  #       force_ssl: [hsts: true]
   119→  #
   120→  # Check `Plug.SSL` for all available options in `force_ssl`.
   121→  config :logger, level: Tuist.Environment.log_level()
   122→
   123→  config :tuist, Tuist.ClickHouseRepo,
   124→    url: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_url(secrets),
   125→    pool_size: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_pool_size(secrets),
   126→    queue_target: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_queue_target(secrets),
   127→    queue_interval: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_queue_interval(secrets),
   128→    settings: [
   129→      readonly: 1,
   130→      # Specifies the join algorithms to use in order of preference: direct (fastest for small tables),
   131→      # parallel_hash (good for medium tables), and hash (fallback for large tables)
   132→      join_algorithm: "direct,parallel_hash,hash"
   133→    ],
   134→    transport_opts: [
   135→      keepalive: true,
   136→      show_econnreset: true,
   137→      inet6: Tuist.Environment.use_ipv6?(secrets)
   138→    ]
   139→
   140→  config :tuist, Tuist.IngestRepo,
   141→    url: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_url(secrets),
   142→    pool_size: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_pool_size(secrets),
   143→    queue_target: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_queue_target(secrets),
   144→    queue_interval: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_queue_interval(secrets),
   145→    flush_interval_ms: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_flush_interval_ms(secrets),
   146→    max_buffer_size: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_max_buffer_size(secrets),
   147→    pool_size: Tuist.Environment.clickhouse_buffer_pool_size(secrets),
   148→    transport_opts: [
   149→      keepalive: true,
   150→      show_econnreset: true,
   151→      inet6: Tuist.Environment.use_ipv6?(secrets)
   152→    ]
   153→
   154→  config :tuist, Tuist.Repo, database_options
   155→
   156→  config :tuist,
   157→    ecto_repos: [Tuist.Repo, Tuist.IngestRepo],
   158→    generators: [timestamp_type: :utc_datetime],
   159→    api_pipeline_producer_module: OffBroadwayMemory.Producer,
   160→    api_pipeline_producer_options: [buffer: :api_data_pipeline_in_memory_buffer]
   161→end
   162→
   163→if env == :dev do
   164→  dev_db_config =
   165→    for {env_var, key} <- [
   166→          {"DATABASE_USERNAME", :username},
   167→          {"DATABASE_PASSWORD", :password},
   168→          {"DATABASE_HOST", :hostname}
   169→        ],
   170→        value = System.get_env(env_var),
   171→        do: {key, value}
   172→
   173→  config :tuist, Tuist.Repo, dev_db_config
   174→end
   175→
   176→if Enum.member?([:prod, :stag, :can, :dev], env) do
   177→  port = "8080"
   178→  app_url = Tuist.Environment.app_url([route_type: :app], secrets)
   179→  %{host: app_url_host, port: app_url_port, scheme: app_url_scheme} = URI.parse(app_url)
   180→
   181→  http_ip =
   182→    case {env, app_url_host} do
   183→      {:dev, "localhost"} -> {127, 0, 0, 1}
   184→      {:dev, _host} -> {0, 0, 0, 0}
   185→      # Enable IPv6 and bind on all interfaces.
   186→      {_env, _host} -> {0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0, 0}
   187→    end
   188→
   189→  check_origin = if env == :dev, do: false, else: [app_url]
   190→
   191→  config :tuist, TuistWeb.Endpoint,
   192→    url: [host: app_url_host, port: app_url_port, scheme: app_url_scheme],
   193→    check_origin: check_origin,
   194→    http: [
   195→      ip: http_ip,
   196→      port: port
   197→    ]
   198→
   199→  # ## Configuring the mailer
   200→  #
   201→  # In production you need to configure the mailer to use a different adapter.
   202→  # Also, you may need to configure the Swoosh API client of your choice if you
   203→  # are not using SMTP. Here is an example of the configuration:
   204→  #
   205→  #     config :tuist, Tuist.Mailer,
   206→  #       adapter: Swoosh.Adapters.Mailgun,
   207→  #       api_key: System.get_env("MAILGUN_API_KEY"),
   208→  #       domain: System.get_env("MAILGUN_DOMAIN")
   209→  #
   210→  # For this example you need include a HTTP client required by Swoosh API client.
   211→  # Swoosh supports Hackney and Finch out of the box:
   212→  #
   213→  #     config :swoosh, :api_client, Swoosh.ApiClient.Hackney
   214→  #
   215→  # See https://hexdocs.pm/swoosh/Swoosh.html#module-installation for details.
   216→end
   217→
   218→if Tuist.Environment.error_tracking_enabled?() do
   219→  config :sentry,
   220→    client: TuistCommon.SentryHTTPClient,
   221→    dsn: Tuist.Environment.sentry_dsn(secrets),
   222→    environment_name: env,
   223→    enable_source_code_context: true,
   224→    root_source_code_paths: [File.cwd!()],
   225→    before_send: {Tuist.SentryEventFilter, :before_send}
   226→end
   227→
   228→# Ex.AWS
   229→if Tuist.Environment.env() not in [:test] do
   230→  %{host: s3_endpoint_host, scheme: s3_scheme, port: s3_port} =
   231→    secrets |> Tuist.Environment.s3_endpoint() |> URI.parse()
   232→
   233→  s3_config =
   234→    then(
   235→      [
   236→        scheme: "#{s3_scheme}://",
   237→        host: s3_endpoint_host,
   238→        region: Tuist.Environment.s3_region(secrets),
   239→        virtual_host: Tuist.Environment.s3_virtual_host(secrets),
   240→        bucket_as_host: Tuist.Environment.s3_bucket_as_host(secrets)
   241→      ],
   242→      &if(is_nil(s3_port), do: &1, else: Keyword.put(&1, :port, s3_port))
   243→    )
   244→
   245→  config :ex_aws, :req_opts,
   246→    # Note: connect_options cannot be used with Finch
   247→    # Connection timeout is handled at the Finch pool level
   248→
   249→    # Maximum time (in ms) that an idle connection can remain in the pool
   250→    # before being closed. Helps prevent stale connections.
   251→    # Set to :infinity to keep connections alive indefinitely
   252→    pool_max_idle_time: Tuist.Environment.s3_pool_max_idle_time(secrets),
   253→
   254→    # Maximum time (in ms) to wait for data after the connection is established
   255→    # This timeout resets each time data is received, so large files can still
   256→    # be downloaded as long as data keeps flowing
   257→    receive_timeout: Tuist.Environment.s3_receive_timeout(secrets),
   258→
   259→    # Maximum time (in ms) to wait when checking out a connection from the pool
   260→    # If all connections are busy, this is how long it will wait for one to
   261→    # become available before timing out
   262→    pool_timeout: Tuist.Environment.s3_pool_timeout(secrets)
   263→
   264→  config :ex_aws, :s3, s3_config
   265→
   266→  config :ex_aws,
   267→    http_client: TuistCommon.AWS.Client
   268→
   269→  config :tuist_common, finch_name: Tuist.Finch
   270→
   271→  case Tuist.Environment.s3_authentication_method(secrets) do
   272→    :env_access_key_id_and_secret_access_key ->
   273→      config :ex_aws,
   274→        secret_access_key: Tuist.Environment.s3_secret_access_key(secrets),
   275→        access_key_id: Tuist.Environment.s3_access_key_id(secrets)
   276→
   277→    :aws_web_identity_token_from_env_vars ->
   278→      config :ex_aws,
   279→        secret_access_key: [{:awscli, "profile_name", 30}],
   280→        access_key_id: [{:awscli, "profile_name", 30}],
   281→        awscli_auth_adapter: ExAws.STS.AuthCache.AssumeRoleWebIdentityAdapter
   282→
   283→    _ ->
   284→      nil
   285→  end
   286→end
   287→
   288→# Stripe config
   289→if Tuist.Environment.stripe_configured?(secrets) do
   290→  config :stripity_stripe,
   291→    api_key: Tuist.Environment.stripe_api_key(secrets),
   292→    signing_secret: Tuist.Environment.stripe_endpoint_secret(secrets)
   293→end
   294→
   295→config :ueberauth, Ueberauth.Strategy.Apple.OAuth,
   296→  client_id: Tuist.Environment.apple_service_client_id(secrets),
   297→  client_secret: {Tuist.OAuth.Apple, :client_secret}
   298→
   299→# Omniauth
   300→config :ueberauth, Ueberauth.Strategy.Github.OAuth,
   301→  client_id: Tuist.Environment.github_app_client_id(secrets),
   302→  client_secret: Tuist.Environment.github_app_client_secret(secrets)
   303→
   304→config :ueberauth, Ueberauth.Strategy.Google.OAuth,
   305→  client_id: Tuist.Environment.google_oauth_client_id(secrets),
   306→  client_secret: Tuist.Environment.google_oauth_client_secret(secrets)
   307→
   308→# Mailgun configuration
   309→if Tuist.Environment.mail_configured?(secrets) and Tuist.Environment.env() in [:prod, :can, :stag] do
   310→  base_uri =
   311→    cond do
   312→      env in [:prod, :can, :stag] -> "https://api.eu.mailgun.net/v3"
   313→      env in [:dev] -> "https://api.mailgun.net/v3"
   314→    end
   315→
   316→  config :tuist, Tuist.Mailer,
   317→    adapter: Bamboo.MailgunAdapter,
   318→    api_key: Tuist.Environment.mailgun_api_key(secrets),
   319→    domain: Tuist.Environment.mailing_domain(secrets),
   320→    base_uri: base_uri
   321→end
   322→
   323→otel_endpoint = Tuist.Environment.get([:otel, :exporter, :otlp, :endpoint])
   324→
   325→# Oban
   326→config :tuist, Oban,
   327→  queues: [default: 10, registry: 2],
   328→  plugins: [
   329→    {Oban.Plugins.Pruner, max_age: 60 * 60 * 24 * 7},
   330→    {Oban.Plugins.Lifeline, rescue_after: to_timeout(minute: 30)},
   331→    {Oban.Plugins.Cron,
   332→     crontab:
   333→       if(Tuist.Environment.tuist_hosted?() and env in [:prod, :stag, :can],
   334→         do: [
   335→           {"0 10 * * 1-5", Tuist.Ops.DailySlackReportWorker},
   336→           {"0 * * * 1-5", Tuist.Ops.HourlySlackReportWorker},
   337→           {"@hourly", Tuist.Registry.Swift.Workers.SyncPackagesWorker},
   338→           {"@hourly", Tuist.Slack.Workers.ReportWorker},
   339→           {"*/10 * * * *", Tuist.Alerts.Workers.AlertWorker},
   340→           {"@daily", Tuist.Billing.Workers.SyncStripeMetersWorker},
   341→           {"@daily", Tuist.Accounts.Workers.UpdateAllAccountsUsageWorker},
   342→           {"@daily", Tuist.Tests.Workers.ClearStaleFlakyFlagsWorker}
   343→         ],
   344→         else: []
   345→       )}
   346→  ]
   347→
   348→# Guardian
   349→config :tuist, Tuist.Guardian,
   350→  issuer: "tuist",
   351→  secret_key: Tuist.Environment.secret_key_tokens(secrets)
   352→
   353→# Prometheus
   354→config :tuist, Tuist.PromEx,
   355→  disabled: not Tuist.Environment.prometheus_enabled?(),
   356→  manual_metrics_start_delay: :no_delay,
   357→  drop_metrics_groups: [],
   358→  grafana: :disabled,
   359→  ets_flush_interval: 20_000,
   360→  metrics_server: [
   361→    port: 9091,
   362→    auth_strategy: :none
   363→  ]
   364→
   365→if otel_endpoint do
   366→  config :opentelemetry,
   367→    span_processor: :batch,
   368→    resource: [
   369→      service: [name: "tuist-server", namespace: "tuist"],
   370→      deployment: [environment: to_string(env)]
   371→    ]
   372→
   373→  config :opentelemetry_exporter,
   374→    otlp_protocol: :grpc,
   375→    otlp_endpoint: otel_endpoint
   376→else
   377→  config :opentelemetry,
   378→    traces_exporter: :none
   379→end
   380→
   381→if Tuist.Environment.analytics_enabled?(secrets) do
   382→  config :posthog,
   383→    api_url: Tuist.Environment.posthog_url(secrets),
   384→    api_key: Tuist.Environment.posthog_api_key(secrets)
   385→
   386→  config :posthog,
   387→    json_library: Jason,
   388→    enabled_capture: true,
   389→    http_client: Tuist.PostHog.HTTPClient,
   390→    http_client_opts: [
   391→      timeout: 5_000,
   392→      retries: 3,
   393→      retry_delay: 1_000
   394→    ]
   395→end
   396→

1→defmodule Tuist.SentryEventFilter do
     2→  @moduledoc """
     3→  Filters events before they are sent to Sentry.
     4→  This module is used to exclude expected errors that are not actionable.
     5→  """
     6→
     7→  @additional_ignored_exceptions [
     8→    TuistWeb.Errors.BadRequestError,
     9→    TuistWeb.Errors.NotFoundError,
    10→    TuistWeb.Errors.TooManyRequestsError,
    11→    TuistWeb.Errors.UnauthorizedError
    12→  ]
    13→
    14→  def before_send(event) do
    15→    TuistCommon.SentryEventFilter.before_send(event, @additional_ignored_exceptions)
    16→  end
    17→end
    18→

1→import Config
     2→
     3→config :cache, Cache.PromEx,
     4→  disabled: false,
     5→  manual_metrics_start_delay: :no_delay,
     6→  drop_metrics_groups: [],
     7→  grafana: :disabled
     8→
     9→config :cache, Cache.Repo,
    10→  busy_timeout: 30_000,
    11→  journal_mode: :wal,
    12→  synchronous: :normal,
    13→  temp_store: :memory,
    14→  queue_target: 1_000,
    15→  queue_interval: 1_000
    16→
    17→config :cache, Cache.SQLiteBuffer,
    18→  flush_interval_ms: 500,
    19→  flush_timeout_ms: 30_000,
    20→  max_batch_size: 1000,
    21→  shutdown_ms: 30_000
    22→
    23→config :cache, CacheWeb.Endpoint,
    24→  url: [host: "localhost"],
    25→  adapter: Bandit.PhoenixAdapter,
    26→  render_errors: [
